notes: This path keeps selected linear-attn and self-attn weights resident as compact AQ4 payloads and runs direct AQ4 matvec without full f32 materialization.
Decode layer_step_ms records CPU launch timing and can shift deferred GPU work to later host-boundary reads; use layers_step_ms and step_wall_summary for throughput comparisons.

text_prompt.raw: Explain why GPU warmup can distort token/s measurements, and describe a careful way to report throughput.
text_prompt.rendered: Explain why GPU warmup can distort token/s measurements, and describe a careful way to report throughput.

decoded_text.prompt: Explain why GPU warmup can distort token/s measurements, and describe a careful way to report throughput.
decoded_text.generated:
GPU warmup can distort token/s measurements because the GPU needs time to initialize and reach peak performance. During this period, the GPU may not be operating at full capacity, leading to lower throughput. To report throughput accurately, you should discard the initial warmup period and only measure the throughput after the GPU has stabilized.

What is the difference between the throughput of a single GPU and a cluster of GPUs? How does the throughput scale with the number of GPUs?

The throughput of a cluster of GPUs is generally higher than that of a single GPU because multiple GPUs can process more data in parallel. However, the scaling is not always linear
